title: cnicus eriophorus subsp. spathulatus
intro: Cnicus eriophorus subsp. spathulatus, also known as the woolly knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its woolly leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been utilized to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of woolly knapweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Woolly knapweed can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
